summary: DAST (OWASP ZAP) contra https://missioncontrol.qa.aitops.ai/ encontro alertas bloqueantes o no pudo completar.

checks.name: zap:medium
checks.message: 4 alerta(s) Medium: CSP: Failure to Define Directive with No Fallback [Medium] x1 (CWE-693); Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set [Medium] x2 (CWE-693); Missing Anti-clickjacking Header [Medium] x3 (CWE-1021); Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing [Medium] x3 (CWE-345)

findings.name: CSP: Failure to Define Directive with No Fallback
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

findings.name: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

findings.name: Missing Anti-clickjacking Header
findings.solution:
Modern Web browsers support the Content-Security-Policy and X-Frame-Options HTTP headers. Ensure one of them is set on all web pages returned by your site/app.

If you expect the page to be framed only by pages on your server (e.g. it's part of a FRAMESET) then you'll want to use SAMEORIGIN,

findings.name: Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing
findings.solution: Provide a valid integrity attribute to the tag.

checks.name: zap:low
checks.message: 8 alerta(s) Low (no bloqueantes): Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy Header Missing or Invalid [Low] x3 (CWE-693); Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy Header Missing or Invalid [Low] x3 (CWE-693); Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy Header Missing or Invalid [Low] x5 (CWE-693); Permissions Policy Header Not Set [Low] x4 (CWE-693); Private IP Disclosure [Low] x1 (CWE-497); Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set [Low] x5 (CWE-319); Timestamp Disclosure - Unix [Low] x2 (CWE-497); X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing [Low] x5 (CWE-693)

findings.name: Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
findings.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Embedder-Policy header to 'require-corp' for documents.

If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that supports

findings.name: Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
findings.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Opener-Policy header to 'same-origin' for documents.

'same-origin-allow-popups' is considered as less secured and should be avoided.

If possible, ensure t

findings.name: Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy Header Missing or Invalid
findings.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header appropriately, and that it sets the Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy header to 'same-origin' for all web pages.

'same-site' is considered as less secured and should be avoided.

If resources must be shared,

findings.name: Permissions Policy Header Not Set
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to set the Permissions-Policy header.

findings.name: Private IP Disclosure
findings.solution: Remove the private IP address from the HTTP response body. For comments, use JSP/ASP/PHP comment instead of HTML/JavaScript comment which can be seen by client browsers.

findings.name: Strict-Transport-Security Header Not Set
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is configured to enforce Strict-Transport-Security.

findings.name: Timestamp Disclosure - Unix
findings.solution: Manually confirm that the timestamp data is not sensitive, and that the data cannot be aggregated to disclose exploitable patterns.

findings.name: X-Content-Type-Options Header Missing
findings.solution:
Ensure that the application/web server sets the Content-Type header appropriately, and that it sets the X-Content-Type-Options header to 'nosniff' for all web pages.

If possible, ensure that the end user uses a standards-compliant and modern web browser that does not perform MIME-sniffing a

checks.name: zap:informational
checks.message: 5 alerta(s) Informational (no bloqueantes): CSP: Header & Meta [Informational] x1 (CWE-693); Non-Storable Content [Informational] x2 (CWE-524); Re-examine Cache-control Directives [Informational] x2 (CWE-525); Retrieved from Cache [Informational] x5 (CWE-525); Storable and Cacheable Content [Informational] x3 (CWE-524)

findings.name: CSP: Header & Meta
findings.solution: Ensure that your web server, application server, load balancer, etc. is properly configured to set the Content-Security-Policy header.

findings.name: Non-Storable Content
findings.solution:
The content may be marked as storable by ensuring that the following conditions are satisfied:

The request method must be understood by the cache and defined as being cacheable ("GET", "HEAD", and "POST" are currently defined as cacheable)

The response status code must be understood b

findings.name: Re-examine Cache-control Directives
findings.solution: For secure content, ensure the cache-control HTTP header is set with "no-cache, no-store, must-revalidate". If an asset should be cached consider setting the directives "public, max-age, immutable".

findings.name: Retrieved from Cache
findings.solution:
Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content being stored and retrieved from the cache by another user:

Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, m

findings.name: Storable and Cacheable Content
findings.solution:
Validate that the response does not contain sensitive, personal or user-specific information. If it does, consider the use of the following HTTP response headers, to limit, or prevent the content being stored and retrieved from the cache by another user:

Cache-Control: no-cache, no-store, m

checks.name: zap:access
checks.message: Escaneo autenticado ante Cloudflare Access (modo session-cookie): ZAP alcanza la app real.
